title: Adapalene
description: A third-generation synthetic retinoid that binds selectively to retinoic acid receptors. It is used in acne treatment for its comedolytic, anti-inflammatory, and cell-regulating properties.
usage: Apply a pea-sized amount to dry, clean skin once daily in the evening. Avoid applying to broken skin. Use sunscreen daily as it increases photosensitivity.

title: Zinc PCA
description: A zinc complex with L-pyrrolidone carboxylic acid that regulates sebum production and provides antimicrobial activity. It is used in formulations targeting oily and acne-prone skin.
usage: Apply zinc PCA serums or toners to oily areas once or twice daily. Commonly found in sebum-control formulations. Suitable for long-term daily use.

title: Phytic Acid
description: A polyhydroxy acid derived from rice bran with chelating and gentle exfoliating properties. It has larger molecular weight than glycolic acid making it suitable for sensitive skin.
usage: Apply phytic acid toner or serum in the evening after cleansing. Start with lower concentrations for gentle exfoliation. Use sunscreen in the morning.
avoid_with: Other strong exfoliants on same night

title: Astaxanthin
description: A carotenoid pigment found in microalgae and marine life with potent antioxidant activity. It protects skin from oxidative stress and UV damage more effectively than many other antioxidants.
usage: Apply astaxanthin serums in the morning for antioxidant protection. Often formulated in oil-based serums. Combine with vitamin C and E for synergistic protection.

title: Avobenzone
description: A chemical UV filter that absorbs the full UVA spectrum, particularly UVA I wavelengths. It is one of the most effective UVA filters but requires photostabilizers to prevent degradation.
usage: Apply avobenzone-containing sunscreen as the final step in the morning routine. Reapply every 2 hours during sun exposure. Look for formulations with photostabilizers.

title: Bisabolol
description: A natural sesquiterpene alcohol derived from chamomile with documented anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties. It enhances penetration of other ingredients while calming irritation.
usage: Found in soothing formulations, post-sun products, and sensitive-skin moisturizers. Apply as part of calming skincare routines. Suitable for twice-daily use.

title: Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate
description: A stable, water-soluble vitamin C derivative that converts to ascorbic acid on the skin. It provides antioxidant benefits and supports collagen synthesis with minimal irritation potential.
usage: Apply SAP serum in the morning before sunscreen. Suitable for sensitive skin. Use at 5-10% concentration for effective results. Store in a cool place.

title: Tocopheryl Acetate
description: A stabilized ester form of vitamin E that converts to active tocopherol on the skin. It provides antioxidant protection and moisturization with improved stability and shelf life.
usage: Found widely in moisturizers, serums, and sunscreens. Apply as part of daily skincare routine. Works synergistically with vitamin C for enhanced photoprotection.

title: Urea
description: A naturally occurring humectant in skin that moisturizes and gently exfoliates at higher concentrations. It helps soften rough, dry skin and supports the natural moisturizing factor.
usage: Use 5-10% urea creams for daily moisturizing and 10-40% for keratolytic effect on rough skin. Apply to dry areas such as feet, elbows, and knees.

title: Sodium PCA
description: The sodium salt of pyrrolidone carboxylic acid, a component of the skin's natural moisturizing factor. It is a powerful humectant that attracts and retains moisture in the stratum corneum.
usage: Found in moisturizers, serums, and toners. Apply as part of the hydrating step in skincare routines. Works well with other humectants like glycerin and hyaluronic acid.

title: Phytosphingosine
description: A sphingolipid naturally found in the skin barrier with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It is a precursor to ceramides and helps maintain healthy barrier function.
usage: Apply phytosphingosine-containing serums or creams to support barrier health. Suitable for acne-prone and sensitive skin. Can be used in both morning and evening.

title: Sea Buckthorn Oil
description: A nutrient-dense oil extracted from Hippophae rhamnoides berries and seeds rich in omega fatty acids, vitamins A, C, E, and carotenoids. It supports skin regeneration and barrier repair.
usage: Apply sea buckthorn oil to clean skin as a treatment oil. Best used in evening routines. A few drops are sufficient. Store in a dark, cool place to preserve nutrients.

title: Tamanu Oil
description: A thick, green oil extracted from Calophyllum inophyllum nuts with documented wound-healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is traditionally used for scar treatment.
usage: Apply tamanu oil directly to scars, blemishes, or damaged skin areas. Use as a spot treatment or mix into moisturizers. Its strong scent and thick texture require dilution.

title: Moringa Oil
description: A lightweight oil extracted from Moringa oleifera seeds rich in oleic acid, behenic acid, and antioxidants. It is highly stable and provides deep moisturization without greasiness.
usage: Apply moringa oil to face and body after cleansing. Absorbs quickly making it suitable for daytime use. Can be used as a makeup remover and facial oil.

title: Safflower Oil
description: A lightweight oil extracted from Carthamus tinctorius seeds with the highest linoleic acid content among common plant oils. It is non-comedogenic and suitable for acne-prone skin.
usage: Apply safflower oil to damp skin as a moisturizer or use as a carrier oil. Its high linoleic acid content makes it ideal for oily and acne-prone skin types.

title: Pumpkin Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Cucurbita pepo seeds rich in zinc, vitamin E, and essential fatty acids. It has a distinctive green color and is used for nourishing and revitalizing skin.
usage: Apply pumpkin seed oil to skin as a nourishing treatment. Can be used in facial oils and body butters. Suitable for dry and mature skin types.

title: Black Cumin Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Nigella sativa seeds containing thymoquinone, a compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is used in traditional skincare for various concerns.
usage: Apply black cumin seed oil diluted in a carrier oil to affected areas. Use in serums and spot treatments. Avoid undiluted application on sensitive skin.

title: Emblica Officinalis Extract
description: An extract from amla fruit with one of the highest natural vitamin C concentrations. It provides antioxidant protection, supports collagen production, and helps brighten skin tone.
usage: Apply amla extract serums or creams in the morning for antioxidant benefits. Often used in brightening formulations alongside other botanical extracts.

title: Scutellaria Baicalensis Extract
description: An extract from Chinese skullcap roots rich in baicalin and baicalein flavonoids with potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties for sensitive and irritated skin.
usage: Apply skullcap extract serums or creams to calm irritated skin. Suitable for sensitive and redness-prone skin. Often included in soothing formulations.

title: Ganoderma Lucidum Extract
description: An extract from reishi mushrooms containing polysaccharides, triterpenoids, and beta-glucans with immunomodulating and antioxidant properties used in anti-aging formulations.
usage: Apply reishi mushroom serums or creams in both morning and evening. Often used in anti-aging and skin-revitalizing formulations. Suitable for daily use.

title: Panax Ginseng Extract
description: An extract from ginseng roots containing ginsenosides with energizing, antioxidant, and anti-aging properties. It is traditionally used in Korean skincare for revitalizing tired skin.
usage: Apply ginseng serums or creams in the morning for revitalizing benefits. Suitable for dull and tired-looking skin. Can be used daily in anti-aging routines.

title: Ginkgo Biloba Extract
description: An extract from ginkgo leaves containing flavonoids and terpenoids with antioxidant and vasodilating properties. It helps improve skin circulation and protect against oxidative damage.
usage: Apply ginkgo biloba serums in the morning for antioxidant benefits. Often included in anti-aging and circulation-boosting formulations.

title: Morus Alba Extract
description: An extract from white mulberry roots and bark containing mulberroside F and kuwanon G that inhibit tyrosinase activity. It is used in brightening formulations for hyperpigmentation.
usage: Apply mulberry extract serums or creams to hyperpigmented areas twice daily. Use consistently for 8-12 weeks. Pair with sunscreen for best results.

title: Dipotassium Glycyrrhizate
description: A salt form of glycyrrhizic acid from licorice root with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It helps calm irritated skin and reduce redness in sensitive formulations.
usage: Found in soothing and sensitive-skin formulations. Apply dipotassium glycyrrhizate-containing products to calm irritated skin. Suitable for daily use on sensitive skin.

title: Hamamelis Virginiana Water
description: A distillate from witch hazel bark and leaves containing tannins with astringent properties. It is used in toners for oily skin but can be drying for sensitive skin types.
usage: Apply alcohol-free witch hazel toner to oily areas after cleansing. Avoid products with high alcohol content. Use once daily on a cotton pad.

title: Kaolin
description: A fine, white clay mineral composed of hydrated aluminum silicate used in cosmetic masks and cleansers. It absorbs excess oil and impurities without over-drying the skin.
usage: Use kaolin clay masks 1-3 times per week. Apply a thin layer to clean skin, leave for 10-15 minutes, and rinse with warm water. Avoid letting it dry completely.

title: Bentonite
description: A volcanic clay rich in minerals that swells when mixed with water and has strong adsorption properties. It is used in masks to draw out impurities and absorb excess sebum.
usage: Mix bentonite clay powder with water or apple cider vinegar to form a paste. Apply as a mask for 10-15 minutes. Do not use metal utensils as they reduce effectiveness.
avoid_with: Dry and sensitive skin for extended contact

title: Glycine Soja Oil
description: A triglyceride oil extracted from soybeans rich in linoleic acid, lecithin, and vitamin E. It provides emollient and skin-conditioning benefits in cosmetic formulations.
usage: Apply soybean oil as a moisturizer or use in oil-based formulations. Suitable for normal to dry skin. Often found in body lotions and facial creams.

title: Hydrolyzed Rice Protein
description: Rice protein broken into smaller peptides that provide film-forming and moisturizing properties on skin and hair. It helps improve hair elasticity and skin softness.
usage: Found in hair conditioners, leave-in treatments, and moisturizers. Apply as part of conditioning routine. Suitable for daily use on hair and skin.

title: Ceramide NP
description: A specific type of ceramide that matches ceramide III found naturally in the stratum corneum. It is essential for maintaining barrier integrity and preventing transepidermal water loss.
usage: Apply ceramide NP-containing moisturizers as the final step in both morning and evening. Look for formulations that include fatty acids and cholesterol for optimal barrier repair.

title: Ceramide AP
description: A ceramide type that corresponds to ceramide VI found in skin. It helps maintain barrier function and is particularly effective when combined with other ceramides and lipids.
usage: Apply ceramide AP-containing products as part of a complete barrier repair routine. Most effective when combined with ceramide NP, cholesterol, and fatty acids.

title: Ceramide EOP
description: A ceramide that corresponds to ceramide I naturally found in the stratum corneum. It is a key component of the skin barrier that helps bind lipids to the cornified envelope.
usage: Use ceramide EOP as part of a multi-ceramide moisturizer for complete barrier repair. Apply twice daily for maintenance of healthy barrier function.

title: Hydrogenated Lecithin
description: A hydrogenated form of lecithin derived from soy or sunflower that functions as an emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. It helps stabilize formulations and supports barrier function.
usage: Used in cream and lotion formulations as an emulsifier and active delivery system. Apply as part of moisturizing products that benefit from enhanced penetration.

title: Sphingolipids
description: A class of lipids that form the structural backbone of ceramides and are essential for skin barrier function. They help maintain hydration and protect against environmental damage.
usage: Apply sphingolipid-containing products as part of barrier repair routines. Found in moisturizers targeting dry and compromised skin. Use twice daily.

title: Lecithin
description: A phospholipid mixture derived from soybeans or egg yolks that acts as an emulsifier and skin conditioning agent. It helps deliver active ingredients and supports skin moisture.
usage: Found in creams, lotions, and liposomal delivery systems. Apply as part of moisturizing products. Enhances the skin feel and stability of formulations.

title: Ethyl Ascorbic Acid
description: A stable vitamin C derivative with an ethyl group attached for enhanced skin penetration and stability. It provides brightening and antioxidant benefits with less irritation than L-ascorbic acid.
usage: Apply ethyl ascorbic acid serum in the morning before sunscreen. Its oil solubility allows it to penetrate deeper into skin layers. Suitable for sensitive skin.

title: N-Acetyl Glucosamine
description: An amino sugar that is a precursor to hyaluronic acid and a component of skin glycosaminoglycans. It helps support moisture retention and may improve skin texture and tone.
usage: Apply N-acetyl glucosamine serums as part of anti-aging and hydrating routines. Works synergistically with niacinamide for enhanced brightening effects.

title: Glutathione
description: A tripeptide antioxidant composed of glutamine, cysteine, and glycine that protects cells from oxidative stress. It supports skin brightening and detoxification processes.
usage: Apply glutathione serums in the evening for antioxidant benefits. Often included in brightening formulations. Use consistently for cumulative effects on skin tone.

title: Lipoic Acid
description: A naturally occurring fatty acid with potent antioxidant properties that are both water and fat soluble. It helps neutralize free radicals in both cellular membranes and aqueous environments.
usage: Apply alpha-lipoic acid serums in the evening. It is sensitive to light so store in dark containers. Start with lower concentrations to assess tolerance.

title: Hydrolyzed Keratin
description: Keratin protein broken into smaller peptides that help strengthen and repair damaged hair. It fills gaps in the hair cuticle improving hair texture and reducing breakage.
usage: Apply keratin-containing hair masks and conditioners after shampooing. Leave on for 5-15 minutes before rinsing. Use weekly or as needed for damaged hair.

title: Hydrolyzed Elastin
description: Elastin protein broken into absorbable peptides that support skin firmness and elasticity. Topical application provides amino acids that may help maintain skin structure.
usage: Apply elastin-containing serums and creams as part of anti-aging routines. Best used in conjunction with collagen peptides for comprehensive structural support.

title: Hydrolyzed Silk
description: Silk fibroin protein broken into small peptides that provide a smooth, silky feel on skin and hair. It forms a protective film that improves moisture retention and softness.
usage: Found in high-end serums, moisturizers, and hair products. Apply silk protein-containing products for a smooth, luxurious feel. Suitable for daily use.

title: Hydrolyzed Oat Protein
description: Oat protein broken into peptides that provide film-forming and moisturizing properties. It helps protect the skin barrier and provides a soothing effect on irritated skin.
usage: Found in soothing creams, lotions, and hair conditioners. Apply oat protein-containing products to dry or irritated skin. Suitable for sensitive skin types.

title: Hydrolyzed Wheat Protein
description: Wheat gluten protein broken into peptides that provide film-forming and conditioning properties. It helps strengthen hair and provides a protective barrier on skin.
usage: Apply hydrolyzed wheat protein in hair conditioners and skin moisturizers. Provides a protective film that helps retain moisture. Suitable for daily use.

title: Prickly Pear Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Opuntia ficus-indica seeds rich in vitamin E, essential fatty acids, and sterols. It is known for its high antioxidant content and nourishing properties.
usage: Apply prickly pear seed oil to clean skin as a treatment oil. A few drops are sufficient for the entire face. Best stored in a cool, dark place.

title: Apricot Kernel Oil
description: A lightweight oil extracted from Prunus armeniaca kernels rich in oleic and linoleic acids. It absorbs quickly providing moisturization without a greasy feel.
usage: Apply apricot kernel oil to skin after cleansing as a moisturizer. Suitable for facial massage. Can be used as a carrier oil for essential oils.

title: Baobab Oil
description: An oil extracted from Adansonia digitata seeds rich in omega-3, omega-6, and omega-9 fatty acids. It absorbs quickly and provides moisturization without greasiness.
usage: Apply baobab oil to face and body after cleansing. Suitable for all skin types including oily skin. Can be used as a daily moisturizer or hair treatment.

title: Chia Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Salvia hispanica seeds with the highest known plant source of omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid. It provides anti-inflammatory and moisturizing benefits.
usage: Apply chia seed oil to damp skin after cleansing. Its high omega-3 content makes it beneficial for inflamed or dry skin. Store in cool, dark conditions.

title: Meadowfoam Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Limnanthes alba seeds with exceptional oxidative stability due to its unique fatty acid profile. It provides long-lasting moisturization without greasiness.
usage: Apply meadowfoam oil to skin as a moisturizer or use in hair products. Its stability makes it ideal for formulations requiring long shelf life.

title: Cranberry Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Vaccinium macrocarpon seeds rich in omega-3, omega-6, and omega-9 fatty acids plus vitamin E. It has a distinctive red color and antioxidant properties.
usage: Apply cranberry seed oil to skin after cleansing. Its balanced fatty acid profile suits most skin types. Use as a facial oil or body moisturizer.

title: Raspberry Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Rubus idaeus seeds containing vitamins A and E, omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids. It has natural UV-absorbing properties and antioxidant benefits.
usage: Apply raspberry seed oil as a facial treatment oil. Can be used in body oils and lip balms. Store away from light to preserve its active compounds.

title: Blackcurrant Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Ribes nigrum seeds rich in gamma-linolenic acid, an anti-inflammatory omega-6 fatty acid. It is used for hormonal skin support and barrier repair.
usage: Apply blackcurrant seed oil to affected areas daily. Particularly beneficial for hormonal and inflammatory skin conditions. Store in cool conditions.

title: Borage Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Borago officinalis seeds with the highest natural GLA content of any plant source. It is used in formulations for dry, inflamed, and aging skin.
usage: Apply borage oil to clean skin in the evening. Often included in sensitive and mature skincare formulations. Use consistently for barrier repair benefits.

title: Pomegranate Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Punica granatum seeds rich in punicic acid, a conjugated linolenic acid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It supports skin regeneration.
usage: Apply pomegranate seed oil to clean skin as a treatment oil. Suitable for mature and damaged skin types. Its deep red color indicates high antioxidant content.

title: Squalene
description: A hydrocarbon lipid naturally produced by human sebaceous glands that is a precursor to squalane. It is more unstable than squalane but provides antioxidant benefits at the skin surface.
usage: Found in natural oil formulations. Less stable than squalane, so products containing squalene require careful formulation with antioxidants. Apply as a moisturizing layer.

title: Guaiazulene
description: A blue-colored sesquiterpene hydrocarbon extracted from chamomile with anti-inflammatory properties. It gives cosmetic products a distinctive blue color and helps calm irritated skin.
usage: Found in soothing serums, creams, and masks with a characteristic blue color. Apply to irritated or sensitive skin for calming benefits.

title: Macadamia Seed Oil
description: An oil extracted from Macadamia ternifolia nuts rich in palmitoleic acid, which is also found in human sebum. It provides deep moisturization with a silky feel.
usage: Apply macadamia oil to face and body as a moisturizer. Its similarity to sebum makes it well-absorbed. Suitable for daily use on dry and mature skin.

title: Camellia Japonica Seed Oil
description: A traditional Japanese beauty oil extracted from tsubaki seeds rich in oleic acid and antioxidants. It absorbs quickly and helps improve skin softness and shine.
usage: Apply camellia oil to face, hair, and body after cleansing. A traditional Japanese skincare staple. Suitable for daily use and all skin types.

title: Kukui Nut Oil
description: An oil extracted from Aleurites moluccanus seeds rich in linoleic and linolenic acids. It is traditionally used in Hawaiian skincare for its moisturizing and soothing properties.
usage: Apply kukui nut oil to dry or sun-exposed skin. Traditional Hawaiian use for after-sun care. Suitable for daily moisturizing of face and body.

title: Tucuma Seed Butter
description: A vegetable butter extracted from Astrocaryum tucuma seeds rich in oleic, palmitic, and myristic acids. It provides emollient properties and is used in lip and body care products.
usage: Apply tucuma butter directly to dry areas or use in lip balms and body creams. It melts at skin temperature for easy application.

title: Murumuru Seed Butter
description: A vegetable butter from Astrocaryum murumuru seeds rich in lauric, myristic, and oleic acids. It has a lighter texture than shea butter and provides deep moisturization.
usage: Apply murumuru butter to skin as a moisturizer or use in hair products. Its lighter texture makes it suitable for facial use. Safe for daily application.

title: Cupuacu Butter
description: A vegetable butter from Theobroma grandiflorum seeds rich in fatty acids, phytosterols, and polyphenols. It provides deep moisturization and has a pleasant fruity aroma.
usage: Apply cupuacu butter to dry skin areas or use in body creams and lip products. Its unique fatty acid profile provides long-lasting moisturization.

title: Illipe Butter
description: A vegetable butter from Shorea stenoptera nuts with a high melting point and rich fatty acid profile. It provides long-lasting moisturization and is used in balms and sticks.
usage: Apply illipe butter in balms, stick products, and body butters. Its high melting point makes it ideal for hot climates and lip products.

title: Almond Butter
description: A vegetable butter from Prunus amygdalus dulcis with a smooth texture rich in essential fatty acids. It provides moisturization and skin softening benefits in cosmetic formulations.
usage: Apply almond butter to dry skin or use in body creams and lip products. Suitable for normal to dry skin as a nourishing moisturizer.

title: Sodium Stearate
description: A sodium salt of stearic acid used as a surfactant and emulsifier in cosmetic products. It helps create solid bar cleansers and stabilizes emulsion formulations.
usage: Found in bar soaps and cleansers. Reacts with water to create a lathering cleansing experience. Rinse thoroughly after use.

title: Decyl Glucoside
description: A mild, non-ionic surfactant derived from coconut and corn glucose. It is one of the gentlest cleansing agents used in sensitive skin and baby care formulations.
usage: Found in gentle cleansers, shampoos, and body washes. Lathers moderately and rinses clean. Ideal for sensitive and dry skin cleansing routines.

title: Coco Glucoside
description: A mild, plant-derived surfactant from coconut and fruit sugars. It creates gentle foam and is used in formulations for sensitive skin and natural cosmetic products.
usage: Found in gentle facial cleansers, body washes, and shampoos. Provides mild foaming action without stripping the skin barrier.

title: Cocamidopropyl Betaine
description: A synthetic surfactant derived from coconut oil that acts as a foam booster and mild cleansing agent. It reduces the irritation potential of primary surfactants in cleansers.
usage: Found in shampoos, body washes, and facial cleansers. Works alongside primary surfactants to improve foam quality and mildness.

title: Sodium Lauroyl Glutamate
description: An amino acid-based surfactant derived from lauric acid and glutamic acid. It provides gentle cleansing with a silky feel and is suitable for sensitive skin.
usage: Found in amino acid cleansers and gentle foaming products. Creates a creamy, silky lather. Rinse thoroughly after use. Suitable for daily cleansing.

title: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate
description: A mild cleansing agent derived from coconut oil that creates a creamy, rich lather. It is the primary surfactant in syndet bars and gentle cleansers.
usage: Found in syndet cleansing bars and liquid cleansers. Provides rich foam without the high pH of traditional soap. Rinse well after use.

title: Bamboo Extract
description: An extract from Bambusa vulgaris containing silica, flavonoids, and amino acids. It provides antioxidant benefits and helps support skin elasticity and hydration.
usage: Found in hydrating serums and moisturizers. Apply bamboo extract products as part of daily skincare routine. Suitable for all skin types.

title: Opuntia Ficus-Indica Extract
description: An extract from prickly pear cactus rich in betalains, flavonoids, and polysaccharides. It provides antioxidant and moisturizing benefits with soothing properties.
usage: Apply cactus extract serums or gels to dehydrated or irritated skin. Suitable for sensitive skin. Can be used in both morning and evening routines.

title: Hibiscus Sabdariffa Extract
description: An extract from hibiscus flowers containing anthocyanins, vitamin C, and alpha-hydroxy acids. It provides gentle exfoliation and antioxidant benefits in natural formulations.
usage: Apply hibiscus extract toners or masks for gentle exfoliation. Can be found in natural brightening formulations. Use sunscreen in the morning as with any AHA.

title: Laminaria Digitata Extract
description: An extract from brown seaweed rich in iodine, fucoidan, and alginates. It provides hydrating, firming, and mineral-rich benefits in marine-based cosmetic formulations.
usage: Found in hydrating and firming serums and sheet masks. Apply Laminaria digitata products as part of a nourishing skincare routine.

title: Chlorella Vulgaris Extract
description: An extract from green microalgae rich in chlorophyll, proteins, vitamins, and minerals. It provides antioxidant and detoxifying benefits in cosmetic formulations.
usage: Apply chlorella extract serums or masks for nourishing and revitalizing benefits. Suitable for dull and tired-looking skin.

title: Spirulina Platensis Extract
description: An extract from blue-green microalgae rich in phycocyanin, proteins, vitamins, and essential amino acids. It provides antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits.
usage: Apply spirulina extract serums or masks for nourishing benefits. Often included in anti-aging and revitalizing formulations.

title: Saccharomyces Ferment Filtrate
description: A filtrate from yeast fermentation rich in amino acids, vitamins, minerals, and peptides. It is a staple in Korean skincare for improving skin texture and hydration.
usage: Apply ferment filtrate essences or toners after cleansing. Pat into skin for absorption. A core step in Korean skincare routines for healthy-looking skin.

title: Bifida Ferment Lysate
description: A lysate of Bifidobacterium probiotic bacteria containing peptides, amino acids, and metabolites. It is used in anti-aging formulations for its skin-repairing properties.
usage: Apply bifida ferment lysate serums or essences in the morning and evening. Often used in anti-aging and barrier-strengthening products.

title: Lactobacillus Ferment
description: A ferment from Lactobacillus bacteria containing enzymes, peptides, and organic acids that support skin exfoliation and microbiome balance.
usage: Apply lactobacillus ferment toners or essences as part of microbiome-friendly skincare. Suitable for daily use to support healthy skin flora.

title: Leuconostoc Ferment Filtrate
description: A ferment filtrate from Leuconostoc bacteria that produces hyaluronic acid and other beneficial metabolites during fermentation. It provides hydration and skin conditioning.
usage: Apply ferment filtrate essences or serums for hydration. Part of the fermented skincare trend in Korean beauty. Suitable for all skin types.

title: Royal Jelly Extract
description: A secretion from honeybee nurse bees rich in proteins, vitamins, and fatty acids. It is used in cosmetics for its nourishing and revitalizing properties.
usage: Apply royal jelly extract serums or creams for nourishing benefits. Often included in anti-aging and revitalizing formulations for mature skin.

title: Vitamin K
description: A fat-soluble vitamin that supports blood clotting and vascular health. In cosmetics, it is used in formulations targeting dark circles and bruising due to its role in circulation.
usage: Apply vitamin K eye creams or serums to the under-eye area in the evening. Use consistently for several weeks to see improvement in dark circle appearance.

title: Pyridoxine
description: Vitamin B6 that plays a role in skin health by supporting amino acid metabolism and oil regulation. It is used in formulations targeting acne and oily skin.
usage: Apply pyridoxine-containing serums or creams to support skin health. Often included in acne and oil-control formulations.

title: Biotin
description: Vitamin B7 that supports keratin production and is essential for healthy hair, skin, and nails. Topical application helps maintain skin and hair health.
usage: Apply biotin-containing serums and hair treatments to support keratin health. Often included in hair-strengthening and nail care formulations.

title: Folic Acid
description: Vitamin B9 that supports cell division and regeneration. In cosmetic formulations, it helps maintain healthy skin cell turnover and supports overall skin health.
usage: Apply folic acid-containing serums and creams as part of anti-aging routines. Supports healthy cell function in the epidermis.

title: Cyanocobalamin
description: A synthetic form of vitamin B12 used in skincare formulations. It supports skin cell metabolism and may help with redness and irritation when applied topically.
usage: Apply vitamin B12 serums or creams to support skin health. Often included in soothing formulations for redness-prone skin.

title: Curcumin
description: A bioactive polyphenol from turmeric roots with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It provides yellow-orange pigment and skin-soothing benefits in cosmetic products.
usage: Apply curcumin-containing serums or masks for antioxidant benefits. Its strong yellow color may temporarily stain skin. Use in rinse-off masks for convenience.

title: Quercetin
description: A flavonoid antioxidant found in many plants with anti-inflammatory and anti-aging properties. It helps protect skin from UV damage and environmental stressors.
usage: Apply quercetin serums in the morning for antioxidant protection. Often combined with vitamin C and ferulic acid for enhanced photoprotection.

title: Silica
description: A naturally occurring mineral compound used in cosmetics as an absorbent, bulking agent, and texture enhancer. It helps control oil and provides a silky feel to formulations.
usage: Found in powders, foundations, and mattifying products. Apply silica-containing products to control shine and improve skin feel.

title: Niacin
description: Vitamin B3 in its niacin form, distinct from niacinamide. It is converted to niacinamide in the body and provides similar benefits for skin health when applied topically.
usage: Apply niacin-containing serums or creams for skin health benefits. Less commonly used than niacinamide but provides similar skin-conditioning properties.

title: Thermal Spring Water
description: Natural spring water containing minerals such as selenium, zinc, copper, and bicarbonates. It is used in soothing mists and formulations for sensitive and reactive skin.
usage: Mist thermal spring water onto face after cleansing or throughout the day to refresh and soothe. Pat gently into skin. Follow with moisturizer.

title: Algae Polysaccharides
description: Complex carbohydrate polymers extracted from seaweed that form a protective film on skin and provide intense hydration. They help retain moisture and protect against environmental stress.
usage: Apply algae polysaccharide serums or masks for deep hydration. Particularly effective in sheet masks and hydrating essences.

title: Natto Gum
description: A polysaccharide produced by Bacillus natto fermentation that has exceptional water-holding capacity. It provides superior hydration and a unique skin-feel in cosmetic formulations.
usage: Apply natto gum-containing serums or masks for intense hydration. Its unique structure holds water and releases it gradually to the skin.

title: Plankton Extract
description: An extract from marine plankton rich in amino acids, minerals, and antioxidant compounds. It provides protective and revitalizing benefits in cosmetic formulations.
usage: Apply plankton extract serums for revitalizing benefits. Often used in anti-aging and marine-based skincare formulations.

title: Padina Pavonica Extract
description: An extract from peacock wrack seaweed known for its ability to maintain skin hydration. It contains sulfated polysaccharides that help regulate moisture levels in the skin.
usage: Apply padina pavonica extracts in hydrating serums and moisturizers. Helps maintain optimal skin hydration levels throughout the day.

title: Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer
description: A cross-linked form of sodium hyaluronate that remains on the skin surface longer providing extended hydration. It creates a breathable film that prevents moisture evaporation.
usage: Apply sodium hyaluronate crosspolymer serums as a final hydrating layer. Its cross-linked structure provides prolonged surface hydration.

title: Hydrolyzed Glycosaminoglycans
description: Hydrolyzed forms of glycosaminoglycans including hyaluronic acid, chondroitin, and dermatan sulfates. They provide multi-level hydration and support skin matrix structure.
usage: Apply hydrolyzed GAG serums for comprehensive hydration. Supports both surface and deeper moisture levels in the skin.

title: Glycogen
description: A polysaccharide that serves as an energy reserve in cells. In cosmetics, it provides moisturizing and conditioning benefits and helps support skin cell energy metabolism.
usage: Apply glycogen-containing serums or creams for skin revitalization. Supports cellular energy processes in the epidermis.

title: Myristic Acid
description: A saturated fatty acid commonly found in nutmeg and palm oils. It is used in cosmetics as a cleansing agent and foam booster in soap and cleanser formulations.
usage: Found in bar soaps and foaming cleansers as part of the fatty acid blend. Rinse thoroughly after use.

title: Hydrogenated Polyisobutene
description: A synthetic hydrocarbon polymer used as an emollient and texture enhancer. It provides a smooth, silky feel and helps improve product spreadability without greasiness.
usage: Found in moisturizers, serums, and makeup primers. Apply as part of the emollient phase of skincare products.
